  "findings": [
    "Isolated metal parts are not properly bonded to the electrical grounding system, creating a shock and electrocution hazard if energized."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "De-energize any nearby circuits that could potentially energize the isolated metal parts.",
    "Install appropriate bonding jumpers to connect the isolated metal parts to the equipment grounding conductor or grounding electrode system."
  ],
  "rationale": "Isolated metal parts that are likely to become energized must be bonded to the equipment grounding system to ensure a low-impedance path for fault current, which facilitates the operation of overcurrent devices. Leaving them isolated violates NEC 250.104 and poses a severe shock hazard.",
  "references": [
    "NEC 250.104",
    "NEC 250.30"
  ]
}
